title: Verify Feedrate Override Setting During Auto Operation on the Visu_MCP_Dual Screen
summary: Verify on the Visu_MCP_Dual tipper machine control panel that the Feedrate Override display is at the documented 100% setting when the tipper is operating in Auto mode. This runbook is limited to observation and verification only; the source notes that adjustment is for maintenance personnel during troubleshooting or maintenance.
when_to_use: Use when you need to confirm whether the Feedrate Override shown on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen matches the documented expected setting during Auto operation of the tipper.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to adjust the Feedrate Override setting.
Do not use this runbook as authorization to change controller settings or maintenance parameters.
Do not use this runbook to verify non-Auto operating modes, because the documented 100% expectation is specifically stated for Auto mode.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Navigate to the Visu_MCP_Dual screen, verify Auto mode status, read the Feedrate Override value, and document whether it matches the documented 100% setting.
Provide access to the operator station HMI if needed and support observation of the current operating state on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
If correction or adjustment is required, maintenance personnel are the role identified by the source as able to adjust Feedrate Override for troubleshooting or maintenance purposes.

safety_notes: This runbook is verification-only and does not include changing settings.

The source states maintenance personnel can adjust Feedrate Override for troubleshooting or maintenance purposes; if adjustment is needed, escalate rather than change the setting within this procedure.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: The tipper HMI must be available on the operator station.
The Visu_MCP_Dual screen must be accessible.
The tipper must be operating in Auto mode if the 100% comparison is to be applied.
external_references: OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual, section 4.1.2.1 Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual screen).

visual_references.description: Operator station display controls showing how to access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen, including F3 mapping.
Primary operator HMI screen for the tipper machine control panel showing AUTO, JOG, REF, CYCLE START, and CYCLE STOP controls.

steps.title: Open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and locate Feedrate Override
steps.purpose: Access the correct tipper control screen and identify the display field to be verified.
steps.instruction: At the operator station HMI, navigate to the Tipper Machine Control Panel screen identified as "Visu_MCP_Dual" and locate the Feedrate Override display on that screen.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is open and the Feedrate Override display is visible for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct tipper machine control panel screen is displayed and the Feedrate Override field can be identified.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed or the Feedrate Override display cannot be located.

actions.label: Open Visu_MCP_Dual
actions.exact_action: Navigate to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen from the operator station HMI.

actions.label: Locate Feedrate Override
actions.exact_action: Locate the Feedrate Override display on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the operator station display controls reference showing F3 mapped to "Visu_MCP_Dual" and screen navigation options.
Use the primary tipper machine control panel screen view to confirm you are on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator station HMI is not accessible.
The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be reached.
The Feedrate Override display cannot be identified on the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The listed control mapping includes F3 to "Visu_MCP_Dual" as the primary screen operators need to use.
The "Feedrate Override" shows the speed of the tipper.

steps.title: Confirm the tipper is in Auto mode
steps.purpose: Ensure the documented 100% expectation is being applied only in the operating mode specified by the source.
steps.instruction: On the same Visu_MCP_Dual screen, confirm that the tipper is operating in Auto mode using the documented AUTO control state and related cycle controls shown on the screen.
steps.expected_result: You can determine whether the tipper is in Auto mode from the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The screen indicates Auto mode is active or the operating state clearly supports that the tipper is in Auto mode.
steps.failure_condition: The operating mode cannot be confirmed from the screen, or the tipper is not in Auto mode.

actions.label: Check AUTO state
actions.exact_action: Confirm the AUTO control state on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

actions.label: Review cycle controls
actions.exact_action: Use the documented CYCLE START/CYCLE STOP controls on the same screen as supporting context for Auto operation state.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look at the AUTO button and the CYCLE START/CYCLE STOP controls on the Visu_MCP_Dual operator station control panel.

stop_or_escalate_if: Auto mode cannot be confirmed from the HMI.
The tipper is not operating in Auto mode, so the documented 100% expectation does not apply for this verification.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The AUTO button runs the tipper in automatic mode. The CYCLE START starts the tipper operation cycle when the system is on "Auto" mode. The CYCLE STOP button stops the tipper operation cycle when the system is in "Auto" mode.

steps.title: Read the Feedrate Override value
steps.purpose: Capture the currently displayed tipper speed value for comparison against the documented Auto-mode expectation.
steps.instruction: Read the Feedrate Override value shown on the Visu_MCP_Dual display while the tipper is in Auto mode.
steps.expected_result: The currently displayed Feedrate Override value is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: A clear Feedrate Override value is visible and can be read from the display.
steps.failure_condition: The value is not visible, not legible, or cannot be confidently read.

actions.label: Read Feedrate Override
actions.exact_action: Read the Feedrate Override value shown on the display.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the Visu_MCP_Dual screen reference while locating and reading the Feedrate Override area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Feedrate Override value is not visible.
The displayed value cannot be read reliably.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Feedrate Override" shows the speed of the tipper.

steps.title: Compare the displayed value to the documented Auto-mode setting
steps.purpose: Determine whether the observed Feedrate Override matches the source-defined expected value for Auto operation.
steps.instruction: Compare the displayed Feedrate Override value to the documented expected setting of 100% for Auto mode.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether the displayed value matches the documented Auto-mode setting.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed Feedrate Override is 100% while the tipper is operating in Auto mode.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed Feedrate Override is not 100% while the tipper is operating in Auto mode.

actions.label: Compare to expected value
actions.exact_action: Compare the displayed Feedrate Override value to the documented Auto mode setting of 100%.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the Visu_MCP_Dual screen reference for the Auto-mode control context while comparing the displayed Feedrate Override value.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed Feedrate Override is not 100% while the tipper is operating in Auto mode.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When the tipper is operating in "Auto" mode, the rate should be set to 100%.

steps.title: Record and escalate a mismatch
steps.purpose: Document an out-of-spec observation and hand off any required corrective action to the role supported by the source.
steps.instruction: If the displayed Feedrate Override is not 100% while the tipper is operating in Auto mode, record the mismatch and escalate for maintenance review rather than attempting adjustment within this procedure.
steps.expected_result: A nonconforming Auto-mode Feedrate Override condition is documented and routed for follow-up.
steps.healthy_condition: Either no mismatch exists, or a mismatch has been recorded and escalated appropriately.
steps.failure_condition: A mismatch is observed but not documented or not escalated, or an unauthorized adjustment is attempted.

actions.label: Record mismatch
actions.exact_action: Record if the displayed value is not 100% while the tipper is operating in Auto mode.

actions.label: Escalate for maintenance review
actions.exact_action: Escalate if Auto mode operation is observed with a Feedrate Override value that does not match the documented 100% setting.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the Visu_MCP_Dual screen reference to identify the operating context associated with the mismatch.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed Feedrate Override is not 100% during Auto operation.
Adjustment appears necessary, because the source reserves adjustment for maintenance personnel.
You cannot determine whether the displayed value reflects a valid operating condition.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When the tipper is operating in "Auto" mode, the rate should be set to 100%.
Maintenance personnel can adjust this for troubleshooting or maintenance purposes.

success_criteria: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is accessed successfully.
Auto mode status is confirmed on the tipper control panel.
The Feedrate Override value is read from the display.
The displayed value is verified against the documented 100% expectation for Auto mode.
Any mismatch is recorded and escalated for maintenance review.
healthy_conditions: The tipper is operating in Auto mode and the Feedrate Override display reads 100%.
The HMI clearly shows the relevant control state and Feedrate Override value for verification.
failure_conditions: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed.
The Feedrate Override value cannot be read.
The Feedrate Override is not 100% while the tipper is operating in Auto mode.
An adjustment is required but the current procedure only supports verification.
escalation_guidance: Escalate to maintenance if the Feedrate Override is not 100% while the tipper is operating in Auto mode.
Escalate to maintenance if correction or adjustment is needed, because the source states maintenance personnel can adjust this for troubleshooting or maintenance purposes.
Escalate if the HMI does not provide enough information to confirm Auto mode or read the Feedrate Override value.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Navigation controls and F3 mapping to access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
Primary tipper machine control panel screen showing AUTO and cycle controls used to verify operating context.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The "Feedrate Override" shows the speed of the tipper. When the tipper is operating in "Auto" mode, the rate should be set to 100%.
Maintenance personnel can adjust this for troubleshooting or maintenance purposes.

The AUTO button runs the tipper in automatic mode. The CYCLE START starts the tipper operation cycle when the system is on "Auto" mode. The CYCLE STOP button stops the tipper operation cycle when the system is in "Auto" mode.
The listed control mapping includes F3 to "Visu_MCP_Dual" as the primary screen operators need to use.

missing_details: The packet does not provide the exact on-screen visual location of the Feedrate Override field within Figure 4-19.
The packet does not provide a documented recording system, log format, or ticketing destination for mismatch documentation.
The packet does not provide a documented escalation contact, team name, or response path beyond maintenance personnel.
The packet does not provide a time estimate for completing this verification.
The packet does not state whether production must be stopped or whether LOTO is required for this verification.
